network.overall_strategy: The synthesis network centers on the experimentally validated cyanosulfidic pathway for L-Proline in surface environments, constructing the pyrrolidine ring via cyanohydrin formation, thiolation-cyclization, reductive deoxygenation, thione-to-nitrile exchange, and nitrile hydrolysis. To ensure environmental interconnection, hydrothermal reactions produce key precursors like formaldehyde and ammonia from CO2, H2, and N2, which are transported to surface pools for further processing into acrolein and 3-aminopropanal. This creates convergent pathways at hub molecules, with bidirectional flow from hydrothermal reducing conditions to surface cyanosulfidic chemistry, culminating in biochemical assembly potential. All core reactions follow Patel et al. (2015); additional precursor reactions are speculative but grounded in general prebiotic geochemistry.

reactions.name: Cyanohydrin formation
reactions.mechanism: Nucleophilic addition of cyanide to the aldehyde carbonyl of 3-aminopropanal, forming the cyanohydrin (α-hydroxynitrile) 4-amino-2-hydroxybutanenitrile.
reactions.reasoning: This is the carbon-carbon bond forming step that extends the C3 aldehyde to the C4 aminonitrile, setting up the chain length needed for pyrrolidine ring formation (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Thiolation and cyclization
reactions.mechanism: H2S attacks the nitrile carbon, converting C≡N to a thioamide (C=S). The terminal amine then performs intramolecular nucleophilic attack on the thioamide carbon, closing the five-membered pyrrolidine ring and releasing NH3.
reactions.reasoning: This one-pot thiolation-cyclization is characteristic of cyanosulfidic chemistry, where H2S activates nitriles toward cyclization (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Reductive deoxygenation
reactions.mechanism: The Cu/H2S reducing system donates electrons to cleave the C-OH bond at C3, producing the fully reduced pyrrolidine-2-thione with loss of water.
reactions.reasoning: Reductive removal of the C3 hydroxyl group using prebiotically plausible Cu/H2S system (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Thione-to-nitrile exchange
reactions.mechanism: Cyanide displaces the thione sulfur at C2 of the pyrrolidine ring. HCN acts as a nucleophile, attacking the electrophilic thioamide carbon, with release of H2S.
reactions.reasoning: This converts a sulfur-containing functional group to a nitrogen-containing one, recycling H2S for the network (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Nitrile hydrolysis
reactions.mechanism: Sequential hydrolysis of the nitrile group: water attacks the nitrile carbon, forming a primary amide intermediate, which undergoes further hydrolysis to the carboxylic acid with release of ammonia.
reactions.reasoning: Completes the conversion of the nitrile to the carboxylate, yielding L-Proline (racemic in synthesis; L-form via later selection) (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Michael addition to form 3-aminopropanal
reactions.mechanism: Conjugate addition of ammonia to the α,β-unsaturated carbonyl of acrolein, adding the amine at the β-position to yield 3-aminopropanal.
reactions.reasoning: Speculative extension necessary to connect acrolein, a prebiotically available unsaturated aldehyde; 3-aminopropanal formation from acrolein + NH3 mentioned as possible route (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Aldol condensation and dehydration to acrolein
reactions.mechanism: Multi-step aldol condensations of formaldehyde form a C3 hydroxy aldehyde intermediate, followed by dehydration to yield acrolein.
reactions.reasoning: Speculative, but grounded in prebiotic formose-like reactions and dehydration in surface environments; acrolein from formaldehyde condensation referenced (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: CO2 reduction to formaldehyde
reactions.mechanism: Fischer-Tropsch-type hydrogenation of CO2 to formaldehyde using H2 on the mineral surface, providing reducing power for carbon fixation.
reactions.reasoning: Speculative but necessary for hydrothermal-surface interconnection; based on known magnetite-catalyzed CO2 reduction to simple organics in vents, analogous to formate/acetate synthesis (e.g., similar to Fischer-Tropsch-type reactions; no direct citation for HCHO, but extends Huber & Wächtershäuser, 1997).

reactions.name: N2 reduction to ammonia
reactions.mechanism: Catalytic reduction of N2 to NH3 using H2 on FeS mineral surfaces, mimicking early nitrogen fixation.
reactions.reasoning: Speculative, but addresses prebiotic ammonia availability; plausible in H2-rich hydrothermal vents with FeS catalysts for N2 activation (no specific citation from provided literature, but necessary for interconnection; analogous to known FeS reductions).

pathways.name: Core cyanosulfidic pathway from 3-aminopropanal
pathways.description: Direct surface synthesis starting from preformed 3-aminopropanal, building the pyrrolidine ring via HCN and H2S intermediates.

pathways.name: Surface route via acrolein
pathways.description: Surface production of 3-aminopropanal from acrolein and ammonia, followed by core cyanosulfidic steps.

pathways.name: Surface route via formaldehyde
pathways.description: Acrolein synthesis from formaldehyde condensation/dehydration, then to 3-aminopropanal and core pathway.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-surface via formaldehyde
pathways.description: Hydrothermal reduction of CO2 to formaldehyde, surface processing to acrolein/3-aminopropanal, then core synthesis.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal ammonia integration
pathways.description: Hydrothermal N2 reduction to ammonia, used in Michael addition, followed by surface core pathway.

pathways.name: Sub-pathway from aminohydroxynitrile hub
pathways.description: Entry at hub intermediate 4-amino-2-hydroxybutanenitrile, proceeding through cyclization and subsequent steps.

pathways.name: Sub-pathway from hydroxypyrrolidine-thione
pathways.description: Starting after ring formation, from 3-hydroxypyrrolidine-2-thione through reduction and exchange.

pathways.name: Sub-pathway from pyrrolidine-thione hub
pathways.description: Entry at reduced ring hub, thione exchange and hydrolysis to proline.

pathways.name: Final hydrolysis sub-pathway
pathways.description: Direct hydrolysis of nitrile precursor to L-Proline.

pathways.name: Full interconnected hydrothermal-surface-biochemical precursor route
pathways.description: Complete network from hydrothermal CO2/N2 reduction, surface processing, to proline synthesis, enabling flow to biochemical peptide assembly.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Core pathway directly matches Patel et al. (2015) with high fidelity; all steps chemically sound and experimentally validated.
suggestions: Explicitly include Cu/H2S agents in rxn_003 for completeness.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Extends core pathway with plausible Michael addition; aligns with literature mention of acrolein + NH3 route.
suggestions: Specify conditions for Michael addition to ensure selectivity.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Adds aldol condensation step; plausible but speculative for direct HCHO to acrolein without specific citation.
suggestions: Provide mechanistic details or citation for HCHO to acrolein conversion.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Good hydrothermal-to-surface flow via formaldehyde; reduction step grounded in prebiotic geochemistry but speculative.
suggestions: Specify mineral catalysts (e.g., Fe-sulfides) for rxn_008.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Integrates hydrothermal NH3 production effectively; N2 fixation plausible in vents.
suggestions: Include catalysts like Fe for rxn_009.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Sub-pathway from key hub; directly supported by literature.
Efficient sub-pathway post-ring formation; reductive step key but requires Cu.
Short sub-pathway with excellent alignment; H2S recycling highlighted.
Final step perfectly matches literature; addresses nitrile to acid conversion.
suggestions: Discuss hydrolysis kinetics in prebiotic context.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Full network pathway shows strong interconnection; speculative precursors slightly lower confidence but grounded.
suggestions: Add citations for hydrothermal precursor steps; specify mineral agents throughout.

evaluation.network_feedback: Excellent network with strong literature alignment for core pathway; good topology and convergence; minor issues with unspecified agents (e.g., Cu in rxn_003) and speculative precursor steps, but overall viable and interconnected.
